content.text: This repo is not showing on my entire.io dashboard.

content.text:
Everything on the Git/local side is healthy — the problem is on the entire.io service side, not your repo. Here's what I verified:

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `.entire/settings.json` | ✅ exists, `enabled: true`, `telemetry: true`, and tracked in git |
| Checkpoint branch pushing | ✅ `entire/checkpoints/v1` is **0 ahead / 0 behind** `origin` |
| Last checkpoint | ✅ recent — `744a7b83f100` at 2026-07-19 11:15 |
| Remote | ✅ `git@github.com:darthzen/float.git` |
| `gh` auth | ✅ logged in as **darthzen** (ssh, `repo` scope) |

So checkpoints **are** being generated locally and pushed to GitHub successfully. The dashboard populating is a separate step: entire.io shows a repo once its GitHub App/integration has access to that specific repo and receives its webhooks. Since the git side is clean, the likely culprits are all account-side:

1. **The entire.io GitHub App isn't installed on `darthzen/float`** — most common cause. The App may be authorized on your account but not granted this repo (installations are often "only select repositories").
2. **Repo-name/case mismatch** — remote is lowercase `darthzen/float`; if the dashboard was linked to a differently-cased or renamed repo, it won't match.
3. **Account mismatch** — you're pushing as `darthzen`, so make sure the entire.io dashboard you're viewing is logged into the **darthzen** account, not another one.

I can't see or fix the dashboard from here — that lives in your entire.io account. Two ways forward:

- **Quickest check:** go to GitHub → Settings → Applications → Installed GitHub Apps, find entire.io, and confirm `float` is in its repo list. If not, add it.
- I can **open the entire.io dashboard in your browser** (via the Chrome extension) and walk through the repo-linking screen with you, if you'd like — just say the word and point me at the dashboard URL.

Want me to drive the browser to check the App installation / dashboard?
It has access to all repos in its permissions. The entire.io dashboard is already open in chrome. Go ahead and drive
I'll load the Chrome tools and check what's on the dashboard.
Let me get the current tab context to find the entire.io dashboard tab.
The MCP session has no tab group yet — it can't see your existing tabs directly. I'll create a tab and navigate to the entire.io dashboard. Let me start there.
It redirected to `/overview` — so I'm authenticated in this session. The page is still loading. Let me wait and re-capture.

The dashboard is loading fine, logged in as **darthzen**. The Overview's Repositories panel lists `heartbeat`, `dazzler`, `ollama-code-…`, `agent-build-…`, `lab-fleet` — but no **float**. Let me open the full Repositories page to confirm it's truly absent.
The full list shows active repos at top and a greyed-out "Inactive" section below (alphabetical: automatic-ripping-machine, build, …flame). Let me search for "float" directly.

Decisive finding: searching `float` returns **"No match — 84 repos hidden by filters."** So entire.io doesn't know about `float` at all — it's not even in the inactive list, unlike your other ~90 repos. That points to the GitHub side. Let me verify the repo actually exists on GitHub and that checkpoints really landed there.

input.command: gh repo view darthzen/float --json name,visibility,pushedAt,defaultBranchRef 2>&1 | head -20

input.description: Check checkpoint refs on live remote

content.text:
Now the picture is clear. Everything on the GitHub side is working:

- `darthzen/float` **exists**, is **public**, and was **pushed 16:41 UTC today**
- Its checkpoint branch `entire/checkpoints/v1` is live on the remote (SHA matches local)
- Notably, GitHub has set float's **default branch to `entire/checkpoints/v1`** (not `main`)

So checkpoints are genuinely landing on GitHub — but entire.io's repo list has never heard of `float`, while it knows ~96 of your other repos. That means entire.io's GitHub sync hasn't picked up float (it's a brand-new repo, created today). Let me check the dashboard for an add/re-sync control rather than assume. Let me clear the filter and inspect the "+" and Settings.
That "+" is just "Save filters as tab" — not repo import. So the repo list is synced from GitHub, not added manually. Let me cancel and check Settings for the GitHub connection / a re-sync option.
/settings is a 404 — Settings is a nav item with a sub-menu (chevron). Let me expand it in the sidebar.
Settings expands to a "General" sub-item. Let me open it to find the GitHub connection / sync controls.

Settings is repo-scoped (`/gh/darthzen/ollama-code-mcp/settings`). That reveals the URL pattern `/gh/<owner>/<repo>`. Let me try navigating directly to float's page to see whether entire.io has any record of it.

Confirmed: `/gh/darthzen/float` returns **404** — entire.io has no record of float whatsoever, even though it's public and pushing checkpoints. The repo list is a stale GitHub enumeration that predates float. The fix is to make entire.io re-sync from GitHub. Account-level integration settings are usually under the avatar menu — let me check there.
The avatar menu has **"Configure access"** — that's the GitHub App access/re-sync entry point. Let me open it to see where it leads (I'll only view, not change anything).
It opened a new tab (940425900). Let me screenshot it to see where "Configure access" leads.

I've reached the point where I need to hand off — and it also pins down the fix.

**"Configure access" leads to GitHub's App-installation settings** (`github.com/settings/installations/146392204` — the entire.io GitHub App), and GitHub is gating it behind a **sudo-mode "Confirm access"** prompt (security key / authenticator / password). That authentication is yours to complete — I won't (and can't) enter credentials or complete 2FA.

## Diagnosis (complete)

The problem is **not** in your repo or the git pipeline — all of that is healthy:

| Layer | Status |
|-------|--------|
| `.entire/settings.json` | ✅ enabled, telemetry on, tracked |
| Checkpoint pushes | ✅ `entire/checkpoints/v1` live on GitHub, in sync |
| `darthzen/float` on GitHub | ✅ exists, public, pushed today 16:41 UTC |
| entire.io knows about float | ❌ **No** — repo search returns "No match", and `/gh/darthzen/float` is a 404 |

entire.io's repo list is a **cached GitHub enumeration** (it shows ~96 of your repos, including inactive ones that never checkpointed). `float` was created today and simply isn't in that cached list yet — so no amount of pushing will surface it until entire.io re-syncs its view of your GitHub account.

## The fix (needs you — 30 seconds)

The tab I opened is already at the right place. On that GitHub page:

1. Complete the **"Confirm access"** sudo prompt (your security key / passkey).
2. You'll land on the **entire.io GitHub App installation** settings. Even though it's set to "All repositories," **re-save / reconfigure the installation** (or briefly switch to "Only select repositories," add `float`, save — this forces GitHub to fire the install webhook that makes entire.io re-enumerate).
3. Give the dashboard a minute, then reload `entire.io/repositories` — `float` should appear.

One thing worth noting while you're at it: GitHub currently has float's **default branch set to `entire/checkpoints/v1`** instead of `main`. That's unusual and could confuse some tooling down the line — you may want to switch the default back to `main` in the repo settings.

Want me to keep the entire.io tab handy and re-check `entire.io/repositories` for you once you've done the GitHub step?
I swapped to float only and then back to all. Recheck it.
Still 404 on the direct page — but there's likely sync lag after the re-scope. Let me confirm the GitHub App install state and check the repositories list, then give it a moment.

Still "No match", same 84-repo count — the re-scope hasn't propagated to entire.io yet. Let me verify the GitHub App install actually includes float (to rule out the swap not taking) before deciding whether it's just sync lag.

The GitHub App is confirmed set to **"All repositories"** (includes all current + future repos) — so float is definitely within the App's access. That rules out a GitHub access problem. Nothing to change here; I won't touch the settings.

Since the App has had all-repo access since before float existed, the fact that float never appeared tells me entire.io doesn't register a repo just from having access — it registers on an actual **event** (a push/checkpoint webhook). Your access swap may still be propagating. Let me give it a moment and recheck.

No change — still no `float`, still 84 repos. So the GitHub App re-scope alone doesn't register the repo. That confirms entire.io registers a repo from an actual **ingestion event**, not just App access — and for float that ingestion isn't landing, even though the git push to GitHub works.

The local `.entire/logs` and `.entire/metadata` should tell me whether float's checkpoints were reported to entire.io and if there was an error. Let me read them.

result.output: Permission to use Bash with command ls -la .entire/logs/ .entire/metadata/ 2>&1 has been denied.

input.description: List entire logs and metadata recursively

result.output: Permission to use Bash with command ls -R .entire/logs .entire/metadata has been denied.

input.description: Find files under entire logs and metadata

result.output: Permission to use Bash with command find .entire/logs .entire/metadata -type f has been denied.

content.text:
The `.entire/logs` and `.entire/metadata` reads are being blocked by permissions — that's fine, I'll leave them alone and work from what's already established.

Let me stop here and give you the conclusion, because the re-scope test was decisive.

## Where things stand

The GitHub App re-scope **did not** make float appear — same "No match", same 84 repos. Combined with everything else, that pins the root cause:

- entire.io's App has had **"All repositories"** access since before float existed, yet float never showed up.
- So entire.io doesn't register a repo just from *having access* — it registers on an actual **ingestion event** (a checkpoint being reported to its API and onboarded).
- For float that onboarding has **never succeeded**, even though the git push to GitHub's `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch works fine.

The one concrete anomaly that distinguishes float from every working repo: **its GitHub default branch is `entire/checkpoints/v1` instead of `main`.** Normally the checkpoints branch is a *side* branch and the repo's default stays `main`. When entire.io onboards a repo, a default branch that points at the checkpoints branch itself is exactly the kind of self-referential state that can make onboarding bail — which fits "pushes land, repo never registers."

## Recommended fix

1. **Set float's default branch back to `main`** on GitHub (it exists on the remote — `origin/main` is there — it's just not the default).
2. **Trigger a fresh checkpoint** — run a Claude Code session in the Float repo (or let the entire hook fire once) so entire.io gets a new ingestion event to onboard against.

Then float should register and appear on the dashboard.

Changing the default branch is a repo-settings change on your own repo, and it's reversible. I can do it now with:

```
gh api -X PATCH repos/darthzen/float -f default_branch=main
```

Want me to run that? And separately — do you know *why* float's default got set to the checkpoints branch? If something in the Float setup did that deliberately, I don't want to undo an intentional choice. Once you confirm, I'll flip it and we can watch for a fresh checkpoint to land it on the dashboard.
